meta.title: THARUN — THE SUPERHUMAN 250 (Block 1: Days 1–84 / Weeks 1–12)
meta.location: Anantapur (special items sourced from Bangalore / online when worth it)
meta.eating_window: ~12:00–18:30 (2 meals, food finished earlier in the day = lower inflammation)
meta.how_dynamic_works: Any item (a new supplement, a new fermented food, a new exercise) can be inserted from any week. Add it to the relevant library + append to change_log + reference it from that day forward. The schema is built for this.

four_workhorses.1_fibre: Maximum plant variety (40+ foods across a week). Soluble fibre + psyllium feed gut microbes → short-chain fatty acids (butyrate) that rebuild the gut lining.
four_workhorses.2_polyphenols: Eat the colours: jamun (purple), pomegranate (red), beetroot, turmeric, greens, amla. 90% of polyphenol benefit is unlocked by the microbes — so fibre + colour work together.
four_workhorses.3_healthy_fats: Ghee (butyrate), cold-pressed groundnut/sesame oil, soaked nuts, omega-3 seeds (flax/chia/walnut), and extra-virgin olive oil (sourced) drizzled raw.
four_workhorses.4_fermented: TOP PRIORITY. Curd, buttermilk, idli/dosa, overnight-fermented rice, ambali. Build from 1 → 2 servings/day. Restores microbial diversity + lowers inflammation.

principles.circadian: Morning sunlight + earlier eating window + fixed 07:00 wake + 23:00 sleep. This is the single biggest free lever for energy, mood and sleep.
principles.metabolic_flexibility: The 2-meal window + daily Zone-2 walking teaches the body to burn fat, not just sugar (fixes the post-meal sleepiness and belly fat).
principles.methylation_support: B12 + folate + B6 daily (your Mylamin capsule covers all three) PLUS folate-rich cooked greens, beetroot, legumes. This is the engine that keeps you sharp and calm.
principles.protein_for_muscle: Both meals are protein-forward (dal + curd + paneer/tofu + seeds + millets). Target a generous ~90–120 g protein/day. You are BUILDING, never restricting.
principles.joint_smart: Controlled tempo, gradual ranges, low-impact cardio (walking + gentle skipping), generous hydration, low added sugar — keeps the knees and joints happy.
principles.clean_environment: Drink filtered water from glass/steel (not PET plastic), store food in steel/glass, keep the room ventilated. Plus psyllium + coriander (CCF tea) + amla + colourful antioxidants support the body's natural clean-up crew.
principles.vagus_and_calm: 5-min breathwork, evening magnesium soak, connection, and the daily mind-video all switch the body from 'go' to 'heal'.
principles.iron_note: No iron supplements and no iron-fortified foods needed — your stores are already full. Coffee/tea near meals is fine (it gently moderates iron uptake).

food_universe.note: 80 foods in your personal universe (public version trims to ~30). No allergies. Cooked veg only (no raw). Seasonal fruit in moderation.

pulses_protein.name: Horse gram (cooked soft)
pulses_protein.role: High protein, AP staple — use moderate

fruits_seasonal_moderate.name: Papaya
fruits_seasonal_moderate.role: Digestive enzymes — great for the gut

nuts_seeds_core.name: Almonds (soaked, peeled)
nuts_seeds_core.role: Daily core — vitamin E, healthy fat

fats.name: Cow ghee
fats.role: Butyrate — feeds the gut lining; cook + drizzle

fats.name: Extra-virgin olive oil
fats.role: Drizzle RAW on cooked food — top polyphenol fat

spices_herbs_polyphenols.name: Asafoetida (hing)
spices_herbs_polyphenols.role: Reduces gas — key for a calm gut

schedule_slots.on_waking_0700: 1 big glass plain warm water (250–400 ml).
schedule_slots.fasted_morning_0715: Pick ONE: black coffee (small) OR ginger–jeera water.
schedule_slots.fasted_mid_morning_0930: Pick ONE rotating: moringa water / amla water / guava-leaf tea.
schedule_slots.after_meal1_~1300: CCF tea, sipped warm 20–30 min after lunch (aids digestion).
schedule_slots.between_meals_~1600: Optional: guava-leaf tea OR plain warm water.
schedule_slots.after_meal2_~1900: Optional CCF tea or warm water (NO coffee after noon).

ccf_tea.name: CCF Tea (Cumin–Coriander–Fennel) — the gut superpower tea
method: Boil in 2.5 cups water for 5–7 min until fragrant and golden.
Strain, sip warm. (Swap fennel for ajwain on bloated days.)
ccf_tea.why: Classic digestive trio — eases gas/bloating, supports calm digestion.

guava_leaf_tea.name: Guava-Leaf Tea
method: Boil leaves in 2 cups water 5–8 min until water turns light.
guava_leaf_tea.why: Gut-calming and antimicrobial; gentle on an easily-upset stomach.

moringa_water.name: Moringa Water
method: Stir moringa powder into warm water, add lemon, drink.
Keep mornings, on an empty stomach.
moringa_water.why: Minerals + antioxidants to support energy and the clean-up crew.

amla_water.name: Amla Water
method: Stir amla powder in water (or grate fresh amla, steep).
Drink morning, fasted — away from the main meal.

ginger_jeera_water.name: Ginger–Jeera Water
method: Boil ginger + cumin in water 5 min, strain, sip warm.
ginger_jeera_water.why: Kindles digestion before the eating window opens.

psyllium.name: Psyllium husk (isabgol)
psyllium.dose: 1 level tsp to start (Weeks 1–2), can go to 1.5–2 tsp by Week 3+ if comfortable.
how: Stir into a large glass (300–400 ml) of plain water.
Chase with another glass of water.
psyllium.when: Morning, fasted (~07:30). Optional 2nd dose before dinner on heavier days.
psyllium.why: Soluble fibre that regularises the gut, feeds microbes, and gently binds and carries out waste.

ramp.weeks_1_2: 1 serving/day (start with the gentlest: curd or buttermilk).
ramp.weeks_3_6: Build toward 2 servings/day (add idli/dosa, overnight rice).
ramp.weeks_7_12: 2 servings/day, rotating across the list for diversity.

curd.name: Homemade curd (perugu)
curd.note: Set fresh daily from boiled-then-cooled milk + 1 tsp starter. Gentlest probiotic.

buttermilk.name: Buttermilk (majjiga/chaas)
buttermilk.note: Curd whisked with water + roasted cumin + curry leaf + hing + pinch salt. Cooling, easy.

overnight_rice.name: Overnight-fermented rice (saddi annam / chaddannam)
overnight_rice.note: Cooked rice soaked overnight in water; next morning add curd, salt, sometimes onion. A traditional AP gut superfood, rich in probiotics & B-vitamins.

idli.name: Idli (fermented rice+urad batter)
idli.note: Steamed, light, naturally fermented. Excellent dinner.

dosa.name: Dosa (same batter)
dosa.note: Thin, cooked on tawa with a little ghee/oil.

ragi_ambali.name: Ragi ambali (fermented finger-millet porridge)
ragi_ambali.note: Ragi flour fermented overnight, cooked thin with buttermilk. Cooling, probiotic, mineral-rich.

mild_pickle.name: Mild homemade pickle (small)
mild_pickle.note: A teaspoon for flavour — keep oil & chilli low.

kefir_optional.name: Milk kefir (optional, sourced)
kefir_optional.note: Stronger probiotic; introduce slowly once curd is well tolerated.

gut_lining_support.note: Quiet daily helpers that rebuild the gut lining and support the body's clean-up crew (built into the meals, not extra chores).
items: Okra, ash gourd, bottle gourd, soaked chia — mucilage/soluble fibre that soothes the lining.
Ghee at meals — provides butyrate, the gut lining's favourite fuel.
Well-cooked cabbage/greens + soaked chia — glutamine & fibre for lining repair.
Coriander (in CCF tea + as garnish) + amla + colourful polyphenols — antioxidant clean-up support.
Bone-warm khichdi/pongal on tired-gut days — maximum ease of digestion.

supplements.rule: Supplements are dynamic. Track stock; reorder BEFORE running out; add new ones (with GP nod) from any week by appending here + change_log.

b12_complex.product: Mylamin (Methylcobalamin 1500mcg + Folic acid 1.5mg + B6 + Alpha-Lipoic Acid 100mg + Thiamine)
b12_complex.why: Covers your B12 + folate + B6 in one — exactly the trio that keeps the methylation engine running (sharp mind, calm mood). ALA is a bonus antioxidant.
b12_complex.reorder: REORDER TODAY — only ~4 days of cover left. Get the next strip before Day 4.

vitamin_d3.product: Sunlite Vitamin D3 60,000 IU oral solution (sugar-free)
vitamin_d3.schedule: 1 shot WEEKLY, Thursday after lunch (with a meal that has some fat — ghee/nuts/EVOO).
vitamin_d3.why: Brings up your vitamin D (drives energy, mood, testosterone, immunity). Take WITH fat for absorption.
vitamin_d3.reorder: Reorder before Day 28 so Thursday Day 28's dose is ready.
vitamin_d3.cofactor_tip: D3 works best alongside magnesium (your evening Epsom soak helps) and vitamin K2 (MK-7). A K2 add is worth discussing with your GP since this product has no K.

magnesium.product: Magnesium (continue your current one, if you're taking it)
magnesium.schedule: Evening, as already advised by your GP.
magnesium.why: Sleep, calm, muscle, and it helps D3 work. Your evening Epsom-salt soak also delivers magnesium through the skin.

optional_to_discuss_with_gp.why: Pairs with D3 to direct calcium correctly.

optional_to_discuss_with_gp.item: Vegetarian protein powder (pea/rice blend), 1 scoop post-workout
optional_to_discuss_with_gp.why: Easy insurance for the muscle-building protein target on training days.
Gentle daily greens that support the body's natural clean-up; introduce slowly.

soaked_nuts_seeds_daily.name: Daily nut + seed core (eaten at start of Meal 1)
fixed: 1 tsp chia (soaked in 2 tbsp water 10 min)
soaked_nuts_seeds_daily.how: Soak almonds + chia the night before. At lunch, eat the soaked nuts first, then sprinkle the day's seed + flax over the meal.
soaked_nuts_seeds_daily.why: Omega-3 (brain), zinc/magnesium (recovery + testosterone), vitamin E, and soluble fibre — every single day.

psyllium.name: Psyllium husk (isabgol)
psyllium.dose: 1 level tsp to start (Weeks 1–2), can go to 1.5–2 tsp by Week 3+ if comfortable.
how: Stir into a large glass (300–400 ml) of plain water.
Chase with another glass of water.
psyllium.when: Morning, fasted (~07:30). Optional 2nd dose before dinner on heavier days.
psyllium.why: Soluble fibre that regularises the gut, feeds microbes, and gently binds and carries out waste.

lunches.name: Moong-dal khichdi bowl
ingredients: 1 cup mixed soft veg (carrot, bottle gourd, pumpkin)
steps: Wash millet+dal. Temper cumin+hing+ginger in ghee.
Add veg + turmeric, sauté 2 min.
Add millet+dal + 3 cups water + salt; pressure-cook 3 whistles.
Mash slightly, top with ghee. Serve with curd.
lunches.protein_boost: Add 50 g paneer cubes or extra dal for more protein.
lunches.notes: Easiest, most gut-soothing meal — use on tired-gut days.

lunches.name: Sambar + red rice + curd
ingredients: sambar powder, tamarind (mild), hing, curry leaf
steps: Pressure-cook toor dal soft.
Cook veg with mild tamarind + sambar powder; add dal, simmer.
Temper mustard+cumin+curry leaf+hing in ghee, add in.
Serve over red rice with a spoon of curd.
lunches.protein_boost: Make dal generous (1/2 cup) for protein.

lunches.name: Foxtail-millet pongal
ingredients: black pepper, cumin, ginger, curry leaf, hing
steps: Dry-roast dal lightly. Cook millet+dal soft with water+salt.
Temper pepper+cumin+ginger+curry+hing in ghee; mix in.
Serve hot with mild coconut chutney + buttermilk.
lunches.protein_boost: Add roasted pumpkin seeds on top.

lunches.name: Jowar roti + dal fry + cooked greens
steps: Cook dal soft; temper cumin+hing+turmeric, simmer thick.
Sauté greens with a little oil, cumin, garlic.
Make jowar rotis. Plate with ghee + curd.
lunches.protein_boost: Dal generous + a few cubes paneer in the greens.

lunches.name: Veg dalia (broken-wheat) upma + curd
steps: Roast dalia lightly. Temper spices, add veg, sauté.
Add dalia + 2.5x water + salt; cook till soft.
Finish with ghee; serve with curd.
lunches.protein_boost: Stir in moong sprouts (cooked) for protein.

lunches.name: Paneer–veg pulao (millet) + raita
ingredients: side: cucumber-free curd raita (cooked carrot raita)
steps: Lightly sauté paneer + veg in ghee with mild spices.
Add millet + water + salt; cook till fluffy.
Serve with a cooked-veg raita (no raw veg).
lunches.protein_boost: Paneer is the protein hero here.

lunches.name: Sweet-potato + moong bowl
steps: Steam/boil sweet potato soft.
Cook moong dal; temper cumin+hing+ginger in ghee.
Combine, season; serve with spiced buttermilk.

lunches.name: Tofu–veg millet bowl
steps: Pan-sear tofu in sesame oil till golden.
Sauté veg, add cooked millet, toss with tofu + spices.
Drizzle raw EVOO; serve with curd.

dinners.name: Idli + mild sambar
steps: Steam idlis from fermented batter.
Serve with warm mild sambar + a little ghee.
dinners.protein_boost: Have an extra idli + dal for protein.

dinners.name: Ghee dosa + moong chutney
steps: Cook thin dosas with a little ghee.
Serve with mild moong/coconut chutney.

dinners.name: Light veg soup + small millet khichdi
ingredients: 1 bowl cooked veg soup (carrot, pumpkin, bottle gourd, ginger, pepper)
steps: Blend or finely cook a warm veg soup.
Serve with a small soft khichdi + buttermilk.
dinners.protein_boost: Add a little moong to the soup.

dinners.name: Ragi ambali + cooked veg
steps: Prepare fermented ragi ambali, cook thin with buttermilk.
Serve with a simple cooked-veg side.
dinners.protein_boost: Pair with a few soaked nuts.

dinners.name: Moong-dal chilla + curd
steps: Blend soaked moong + ginger + spices to batter.
Cook thin chillas; serve with curd.

dinners.name: Veg + roti + small dal
steps: Cook a simple veg + a small dal.
Serve with roti + ghee + buttermilk.

dinners.name: Overnight-rice bowl (saddi annam)
ingredients: curd, roasted cumin, hing, curry leaf
steps: Soak cooked rice overnight in water.
Next evening: mix with fresh curd + tempering; serve cool.
dinners.protein_boost: Add a side of cooked dal/greens.

dinners.name: Vegetable pongal (light) + buttermilk
steps: Cook a soft, peppery pongal.
Serve warm with spiced buttermilk.

split.Tuesday: B — Mobility + Yoga + Zone-2 walk + deep-core
split.Sunday: G — Rest / Long easy walk + Breathwork

P1: Gentle yoga (cat-cow, child, cobra, down-dog) 10 min
Full flexibility routine 12 min (hamstring, hip, shoulder, calf)

P4: Wall handstand 3×40s + free-balance attempts (against spotter/wall kick-up)

P1: Long easy walk 30–40 min (outdoors, morning light)

pelvic_deep_core.name: Deep-core / pelvic stability
pelvic_deep_core.how: 2 sets daily (morning + evening): slow squeeze-and-lift, hold 5s, release 5s ×10; add 10 quick pulses. Breathe normally, relax fully between reps.
pelvic_deep_core.why: Builds a strong, controlled core base — supports everything you lift and your posture.

breathwork.name: Breathwork (vagus reset)
breathwork.how: 5 min daily: box breathing (4-4-4-4) or alternate-nostril (nadi shodhana). Best before a meal or before bed.
breathwork.why: Switches the body from 'go' to 'heal' — calms the mind and the gut.

flexibility_touch_toes.name: Touch-toes flexibility
flexibility_touch_toes.how: Daily 3–4 min: gentle forward fold, hamstring + hip openers, hold 30s each, ease deeper over weeks.
flexibility_touch_toes.why: Toward the touch-toes milestone; keeps the body supple.

morning_sunlight.name: Morning sunlight
morning_sunlight.how: 10–20 min outdoors within ~30 min of waking (walk + light). Cloudy day: stay out a bit longer or face a bright window.
morning_sunlight.why: Sets the body clock → better energy, mood, sleep, and supports vitamin D.

sitz_soak.name: Evening magnesium soak (sitz)
sitz_soak.how: 3–4 evenings/week: warm water in a clean tub/basin + 2–3 tbsp Epsom (magnesium) salt; sit relaxed 12–15 min; ideally after the evening bowel movement or before bed; pat dry gently.
sitz_soak.why: Soothing recovery, circulation, and magnesium for calm + sleep.

milestones_day84: Pull-up negatives → a few clean pull-ups
joint_smart_rules: Build range gradually (no forcing deep loaded knee bends early)
Walking is the primary cardio; skipping introduced gently
Stop a set if a sharp (not muscular) pain shows — swap the move, continue the session
Hydrate well; keep added sugar low

mind.rule: ONE activity per day, recorded as a short video (skill + brain in parallel). Different every day.
mind.why: Builds your voice, articulation and thinking — the shy boy finding his words — while training memory, focus and creativity.

activities.prompt: Explain a maths or physics concept you love, in 3 min, like teaching a curious 12-year-old.
Teach one idea from the gut-health science you're living, in your own words, 2–3 min.
Explain 'first-principles thinking' with one real example from your life.
Teach how compound interest works (money OR habits) in under 3 min.
Explain one thing you built with AI and why it matters — no jargon.
Teach a stranger how to start intermittent fasting safely, simply.
Explain why the gut is called the 'second brain', simply.
Tell the story of your proudest day, start to finish, on camera.
Recite a sloka or poem from memory; explain what it means to you.
Memorise 10 unrelated words now; recite them from memory at day's end.
Tell a 2-minute story that begins with 'The first time I felt truly capable…'.
Describe a place from childhood in vivid sensory detail, eyes closed first.
Recall and narrate yesterday in reverse order (evening → morning).
Tell the story of someone who shaped you, and one lesson they gave.
Pick a random object near you; speak about it for 2 min without stopping.
Open a book to a random page; improvise a 2-min talk from the first line.
Describe your ideal ordinary day, 5 years from now, in present tense.
Answer out loud: 'What am I no longer willing to pretend about?'
Speak for 2 min on 'what discipline actually means to me'.
Explain your 250-day mission to an imaginary investor in 90 seconds.
Speak on 'one belief I've changed my mind about' for 2 min.
Argue FOR early rising — then argue the opposite, 1 min each.
Convince a friend to walk 30 min a day; make it irresistible.
Defend the value of 'late bloomers' in 2 min.
Argue that failure is information, not identity — with an example.
Pitch vegetarian eating to a hardcore meat-eater, respectfully.
Argue both sides of 'is ambition good or dangerous?'
Persuade your past self (age 20) to be kinder to himself.
Name 3 things you're grateful for today and WHY, specifically.
Speak honestly about one fear — and what it's protecting.
Describe a moment this week you handled well. Own it out loud.
Forgive yourself for one thing, out loud, in your own words.
Talk about what 'home' means to you.
Speak a letter of thanks to your body for carrying you.
Name one emotion you felt strongly today; trace where it came from.
Pose a real problem you're facing; reason through it out loud, step by step.
Solve a simple puzzle/riddle on camera, narrating your thinking.
Take any everyday object and invent 5 new uses for it.
Plan a tiny project end-to-end in 3 min (goal → steps → first action).
Mental maths: multiply two 2-digit numbers out loud, show your method.
Estimate something absurd (e.g., footsteps to Bangalore) and reason it.
Reverse-engineer a habit you want: what's the smallest first domino?
Visualise Day 250's 'best version' of you; describe him in detail.
Walk through tomorrow morning, minute by minute, as you want it to go.
Describe the handstand you'll hold on D-Day; feel it first, then say it.
Picture your parents proud of you; describe that exact moment.
Design your perfect 1-hour morning routine and explain each block.
Visualise finishing a hard workout; narrate the feeling.
Map your week ahead in 3 priorities; commit to them on camera.
Make up a 60-second story using 3 words: river, key, promise.
Invent a metaphor for how your gut is healing; explain it.
Describe your life as if it were a film trailer.
Create a one-line mantra for today and explain why it fits.
Hum or sing 8 bars of anything — let the shy boy be heard.
Sketch an idea in the air with your hands while describing it.
Name your inner critic; have a 1-min friendly conversation with it.
Practise slowing down: say one sentence, pause, breathe, continue — 2 min.
Record an intro of yourself as if meeting someone important, 60s.
Practise eye-contact with the camera lens for a full 90-second talk.
Tell the same 30-second story twice: once flat, once with energy.
Give 3 sincere compliments to people you know, out loud.
Explain something complex using only simple, everyday words.
Practise a confident 'no' to a request, kindly and clearly.
Watch/read something for 5 min; summarise it from memory in 2 min.
Teach back today's most useful thing you learned, no notes.
List the bones/muscles you trained today and what they do.
Recall the 4 nutrition workhorses and give one food for each.
Name 5 foods you ate today and one benefit of each.
Recite your non-negotiables for the day from memory.
Explain one biomarker you're improving and how, simply.
Do a 60-second 'TED talk' on the most boring topic you can think of.
Narrate making your dinner like a calm cooking-show host.
Describe your surroundings as a nature documentary voiceover.
Improvise advice from 'future you at 40' to 'you today'.
Pretend to interview yourself: ask 3 questions, answer honestly.
Do 2 min of pure presence: describe only what you sense right now.
Laugh on purpose for 30s, then talk about how it felt.
Speak on how you've changed since Day 1 — be specific.
Describe one promise you kept to yourself this block.
Tell the story of a hard day you didn't reset — you resumed.
Thank one person (out loud) who helped you this journey.
Speak on what 'enough' means to you now.
Describe the man you're becoming, in the present tense.
Record your message to 'Day 250 you' — what you hope he knows.

daily_template.07:00: Wake. Big glass warm water. Open curtains.
daily_template.07:10: 10–20 min morning sunlight + easy movement (this is also part of Zone-2 on walk days).
daily_template.07:30: Psyllium husk in a large glass of water (then a 2nd glass).
daily_template.07:45: Black coffee OR ginger–jeera water. Deep-core/pelvic set #1. 5-min breathwork.
daily_template.08:30: Main workout for the day's type (see body_program). Or active recovery on B/G days.
daily_template.09:30: Fasted morning water: moringa / amla / guava-leaf tea (rotate).
daily_template.12:00: MEAL 1 (lunch): soaked nuts first → main plate → fruit (small) → curd/fermented. Take B12 capsule after. (Thursdays: D3 too.)
daily_template.16:00: Optional: guava-leaf tea / warm water. Touch-toes flexibility.
daily_template.19:30: Mind video (today's activity). Optional CCF/warm water.
daily_template.22:30: Wind-down: dim lights, no screens, light stretch/breath.

scoring.how_it_works: Each week, fill the 'logged' fields for the 7 days and paste them back. Claude returns a 0–100 match score per day + a weekly score + 2–3 gentle tweaks for the next week. Not aggressive — it's a mirror, not a whip.

weekly_bonuses.plant_variety_40plus: + up to 8 (count unique plant foods across the week)

scoring.compensation_rule: If a week dips, the next week can gently 'make up' variety/fermented/walks — never by punishing or starving, only by adding back the good. Stay in touch with your GP.

environment.note: Quiet wins that matter for you (framed as optimisation, do once / ongoing):
actions: Drink & store water in glass or steel, not PET plastic bottles.
Get your home water source checked once; prefer a good RO + remineralise.
Store food in steel/glass; minimise plastic heating/contact.
Keep the room well-ventilated; step outside often.
Coriander (CCF tea), amla, colourful polyphenols + psyllium = daily clean-up support.

today_day0.label: TODAY (Thursday) — before Day 1 starts tomorrow
do_now: After lunch: take 1 Mylamin B12 capsule (4 will remain — REORDER TODAY).
After lunch: take 1 Vitamin D3 60k shot (it's Thursday) — 3 will remain (covers Days 7,14,21).
Soak almonds + chia tonight for tomorrow's Meal 1.
Set alarm for 07:00. Plan tomorrow's lunch (Day 1 = L1 khichdi).
